    • Market Overview

      The market for properties for sale in Toronto continues to attract attention from buyers and investors who want a clear view of value, selection, and timing. Right now, there are 5,565 active listings available, creating a strong snapshot of current inventory and giving shoppers a practical range of options to compare. The average listing price sits at $802,467, offering a helpful benchmark for evaluating individual homes based on size, condition, and location within the area. On average, properties are spending about 56 days on the market, which can indicate how quickly well-priced homes are moving.

      Recent movement shows +18.2%, suggesting the market may be trending upward or downward depending on the direction of that change. For anyone considering a move, this combination of pricing, selection, and market pace provides useful context: when inventory is competitive, preparation matters; when selection is broader, buyers often gain more flexibility. Either way, tracking these indicators helps set realistic expectations and supports smarter decisions when exploring properties for sale in this part of Canada.

      Browse Listings

      Explore 5,565 available properties for sale in Toronto to get a feel for what is currently on the market and how options compare across different styles and price points. Listings typically range from efficient layouts suited to first-time buyers to larger, more feature-rich homes for growing households, depending on the mix of properties available at any given time.

      As shoppers browse, it can help to focus on priorities such as layout, outdoor space, parking, building features, and proximity to everyday conveniences. Because these homes are for sale, visitors can filter by bedroom count, budget, and preferred property type to narrow the search quickly. With a clear shortlist, it becomes easier to book showings, compare value, and move confidently when the right opportunity appears.

      Pricing Insights

      Pricing for properties for sale in Toronto is shaped by a blend of property type, size, condition, and micro-location. The current average listing price of $802,467 offers a useful midpoint for comparing what’s available today, while the average price per square foot of $858 provides a more apples-to-apples way to assess value across different layouts and building styles.

      Square footage can also play a major role in perceived value. With an average size of 936 sqft, shoppers can better estimate what their budget may deliver in terms of livable space. Since the most competitive listings often attract attention quickly, comparing price per square foot alongside overall price is a practical way to judge whether a property is aligned with market expectations. For a clearer breakdown by bedroom count, the section below highlights how pricing can differ across common home configurations.

      Average Prices by Bedroom

      BedroomsAvg. PriceListings
      Studio$426,277133
      1 Bed$520,2601,021
      1+Den$593,2531,090
      2 Beds$801,9741,655
      2+Den$1,000,966736
      3+ Beds$1,248,041921

      Living in the Area

      Living in Toronto appeals to people who want a balance of day-to-day convenience and a distinct neighbourhood feel. The area’s overall character is often shaped by a mix of residential streets, pockets of local services, and accessible routes that help residents connect to the broader region. Depending on the specific part of the community, walkability can range from highly convenient to more car-oriented, with many residents choosing what best matches their lifestyle—whether that’s being close to everyday errands, having more privacy, or prioritizing space.

      A strong sense of community is one of the main reasons people consider making a move. Neighbourhoods like this often support a rhythm of local life—casual meetups, regular routines, and familiar faces—without requiring constant travel for essentials. Buyers frequently look for a combination of comfort and practicality: functional floor plans, storage, outdoor areas, and a setting that feels welcoming year-round.

      For households at different life stages, Toronto can work in different ways. Some may value a low-maintenance home base that supports a busy schedule, while others may prioritize room to grow, flexible spaces for work-from-home needs, or a quieter residential setting. Exploring homes in person and comparing sub-areas is often the best way to match preferences—such as noise levels, street activity, and the overall “feel”—to the right property.

      Market Trends

      To understand how properties for sale in Toronto is performing over time, it helps to compare key indicators side by side. The table below summarizes recent market trends and can guide pricing and timing decisions.

      Market Trends

      PeriodAvg. PriceListingsAvg. DOM
      Current$802,4675,56556 days
      Last 12 Months$767,66415,44430 days
      Last 90 Days$727,2723,62737 days

      Top Areas

      Toronto often includes multiple pockets and sub-areas, each with its own vibe, housing mix, and price patterns. Reviewing the top areas below can help buyers focus their search and quickly identify where listings best match their needs.

      Frequently Asked Questions

      What is the average price of properties for sale in Toronto?

      The average listing price for properties for sale in Toronto is currently $802,467. This figure is a helpful benchmark, but individual prices can vary based on factors such as size, layout, condition, and where the property is located within the area. To compare value more precisely, many buyers also review $858 and confirm how a home’s size relates to the local average of 936 sqft.

      How many properties for sale are available in Toronto right now?

      There are 5,565 active listings in Toronto at the moment. Inventory levels can change quickly, especially in markets where well-priced properties attract attention. Buyers may want to set up saved searches and alerts so new listings and price changes are easier to track as soon as they appear.

      How long do properties typically take to sell in Toronto?

      On average, listings spend about 56 days on the market in Toronto. Days on market can vary depending on the property’s price point, presentation, and demand for specific home types. A home that is priced in line with comparable listings and shows well may move faster, while unique layouts or higher-priced options may take longer to find the right buyer. Shoppers can narrow this range by prioritizing must-haves—such as bedroom count (including 3+-bedroom options), interior space, and lifestyle preferences—then comparing similar listings to understand what represents good value in today’s market.

      There are a lot of great restaurants nearby.Grab your morning coffee at Tim Hortons located at 2075 Bayview Ave. For grabbing your groceries, Whole Foods Market is only an 8 minute walk.

      If you are reliant on transit, don't fear, there is a Bus Stop (Raab Blvd at Hospital Rd West Side (K & L Wings)) a short walk. It also has route Bayview, and route Sunnybrook nearby. For drivers, it might be easier to get around the city getting on or off Don Valley Parkway and Wynford Dr, which is within a few minutes drive.
